verdict.note: Stale cause is the harness-adapter refactor (bcba4b2/4874864): isOccupying now routes liveness through harnessById(s.harness).liveness() instead of a direct tmux check. The SLOT SET is UNCHANGED — OCCUPIES_SLOT = {working, parked, starting} (sessions.ts:743); every waiting-on-human state (idle/asking/review/done/close-pending) and offline/queued frees its slot exactly as before. For the claude path the adapter's liveness == the old tmux-alive check, so the asserted board behavior (only working+parked occupy; queued drains when working+parked < N) is behavior-equivalent. Not re-run through a live 6-agent board: spinning up 2 working + 4 asking + queued real agents is disproportionate machine load (the main session shares this box) for a refactor that provably does not alter the occupancy set. Code-verified behavior-equivalent.

Same harness-adapter refactor staled this; the cap VALUE path is untouched. maxActive() (sessions.ts:54-62) reads spexcode.json sessions.maxActive first, else SPEXCODE_MAX_ACTIVE env, else default 6, then Math.max(1, floor(...)) — exactly the specced precedence + floor-to-1. drainQueue reads it once per pass (live, no restart). The refactor only changed how occupancy is counted, not how the cap value is resolved. Not re-run via a live edit-the-json-watch-the-drain loop (needs a live multi-agent board); code-verified the precedence/floor/live-read contract holds verbatim.

Real /api/board-status truth on isolated :8833 backend (stub launcher, rvSock touched to make agents genuinely online): cap=2 → two working+online agents hold both slots and a third launch lands queued, while an asking (alive+online) and an offline session hold none; flipping one working agent to asking via the real mark-active hook freed the slot and the drainer launched the queued session within a tick (queued→starting). Slots = compute (working/parked), not live processes.

Backend started with no sessions key (default 6 — three creations never queued); live-edited spexcode.json sessions.maxActive=2 with NO restart → immediately enforced (next create queued); raised 2→3 live → queued session drained on the next tick; lowered 3→1 live → new session stayed queued while the 2 running agents kept their slots (never killed). Restarted with the JSON key REMOVED + SPEXCODE_MAX_ACTIVE=1 → env governed (queue held at 1). JSON wins, env fills in, nothing baked in.
Current launch retry stderr guesses a daemon race for any fast launcher exit.
Fast-exit retry keeps retrying but no longer names an unproven daemon race.
Fast-exit retry keeps retrying but no longer names an unproven daemon race.
Fast-exit retry keeps retrying but no longer names an unproven daemon race.

Measured through the real board (/api/sessions) on a throwaway backend, cap=2 (spexcode.json). 2 working+online filled both slots; s3 held queued across drain ticks. Flip s1->asking (still LIVE) -> s3 launched within one tick. Flip s2->review, create s4 -> launched within a tick though 3 sessions were live (only 1 working). parked OCCUPIES: with 1 parked + 1 working (4 live panes total), s5+s6 stayed queued across 3 ticks. Queue count tracked working+parked, never total-alive; drain fired the instant a slot freed.

All precedence legs behavioral, no restart between edits: (1) json wins — backend env SPEXCODE_MAX_ACTIVE=9 yet json maxActive=2 governed (3rd session queued). (2) live raise 2->4: both queued sessions launched on the next tick. (3) live lower 4->1: all running listeners stayed alive (no kills), new session queued. (4) env fallback: json key removed + env=1 -> new session queued behind the working set. (5) default: no json, no env -> bracketed cap at EXACTLY 8 (occupied=8 -> s10 queued across ticks; freeing one slot -> s10 launched). (6) floor: json maxActive=0 -> with occupied=1 new session queued (cap 1), with occupied=0 it launched — floored to 1, never wedged to 0.

Real backend-generated launch.sh (session created via POST /api/sessions with launcher fastexit=/bin/false), run as the worker pane would, stderr captured: exactly 3 attempts, each line '[spex launch] attempt N exited in 0s (rc=1) - fast launcher exit before readiness; retrying' — reports only the observed condition; no 'daemon race'/'likely' cause claim anywhere; loop bounded (exit rc=1 after attempt 3).
